'''Michael Dobson''' (born 29 May 1986) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. He previously played for Australian [[National Rugby League|NRL]] clubs [[Canberra Raiders]] and [[Newcastle Knights]], and European [[Super League]] clubs [[Catalans Dragons]], [[Wigan Warriors]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Salford Red Devils]]. He primarily played as a {{rlp|hb}} or {{rlp|fe}}. In 2009, he won the [[Albert Goldthorpe Medal]].

==Background==
Born in [[Junee|Junee, New South Wales]], Dobson attended Erindale College and played his junior rugby league for the [[Junee Diesels]], before being signed by the [[Canberra Raiders]]. In 2003 and 2004, Dobson played for the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]].<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|access-date=2008-10-10|publisher=SportingPulse}}</ref>

==Professional playing career==
===Catalans Dragons===
In 2006, the Canberra Raiders loaned Dobson to French club [[Catalans Dragons]] of [[Super League]] as a replacement for injured halfback [[Stacey Jones]].

===Wigan Warriors===
After impressing at the Catalans Dragons, Dobson attracted the attention of several other [[Super League]] clubs, subsequently being signed by the [[Wigan Warriors]] in May 2006. There was some controversy over Dobson's signing for the Wigan Warriors. Under English law, Dobson was not permitted to play in England because he did not have a work permit. However, the Wigan Warriors managed to obtain a work permit for him, albeit in controversial circumstances.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2006/may/23/rugbyleague.sport|title=Rugby league: Desperate Wigan chase work permit for Dobson|website=[[The Guardian]]|date=23 May 2006}}</ref> When Dobson signed for the Wigan Warriors, they were at the bottom of the [[Super League]] table, 6-points behind the nearest team and heading towards relegation. Dobson's impact at the Wigan Warriors helped the team survive relegation in the 2006 season.

In 2006, Dobson was statistically the best goal-kicker in the world with a success rate of 84%, equal to or better than [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]' [[Jamie Lyon]], 81% and [[National Rugby League]] players [[Hazem El Masri]], 84% and [[Andrew Johns]], 80%.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}
[[File:Mick Dobson.JPG|thumb|left|Dobson playing for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in 2010.]]
[[File:michaeldobson.jpg|thumb|right|Dobson playing for the Knights in 2014]]

===Canberra Raiders===
At the end of 2006, Dobson rejoined the [[Canberra Raiders]]. In 2007, Dobson was the club's leading point-scorer. In April 2008, Dobson signed a {{frac|2|1|2}}-year contract with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] effective immediately after being released from his Raiders contract.<ref name="Hull KR win race to sign Dobson">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull_kr/7376027.stm|title=Hull KR win race to sign Dobson|publisher=BBC|date=2008-04-30|access-date=2008-05-01}}</ref><ref name="Dobson completes switch to Robins">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull_kr/7384713.stm|title=Dobson completes switch to Robins|publisher=BBC|date=2008-05-07|access-date=2008-05-07}}</ref><ref name="Dobson set to make Hull KR debut">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull_kr/7415481.stm|title=Dobson Dobson set to make Hull KR debut|publisher=BBC|date=2008-05-22|access-date=2008-05-22}}</ref><ref name="Rovers in pole position for Dobson">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/08/04/30/RUGBYL_Hull_KR.html|title=Rovers in pole position for Dobson|publisher=Sportinglife|date=2008-04-30|access-date=2008-04-30}}</ref> He was also linked to [[Super League]] clubs [[Hull F.C.]] and [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]].<ref name="Hull close in on Dobson signing">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/7363243.stm|title=Hull close in on Dobson signing|publisher=BBC|date=2008-04-23|access-date=2008-04-23}}</ref><ref name="Quick release on cards for Noddy">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23583348-23214,00.html|title=Quick release on cards for Noddy|publisher=Fox Sports|date=2008-04-23|access-date=2008-04-23}}</ref><ref name="Sharp eyes up Dobson">{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3414346,00.html|title=Sharp eyes up Dobson|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-04-23|access-date=2008-04-23}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
In 2009, Dobson helped Hull Kingston Rovers into fourth place in only their third [[Super League]] season. His individual performances saw him become the second winner of the [[Albert Goldthorpe Medal]] which is presented by ''[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]]''.<ref>{{cite web|title=The Albert Goldthorpe Awards|url=http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour/|work=totalrl.com|publisher=League Publications|access-date=16 February 2014}}</ref>

Dobson started the 2010 season with a try and five goals in a 30–12 win over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://rleague.com/players/Michael-Dobson_809/Matches/ |title=Michael Dobson player results, scores, news and statistics |access-date=2013-08-03 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130820081755/http://rleague.com/players/Michael-Dobson_809/Matches/ |archive-date=20 August 2013}}</ref>

In 2013, Dobson played for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] against [[England national rugby league team|England]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/international-origin-2013/game-1/england-vs-exiles/summary.html|title=International Origin 2013 Series – Game – England 30 def. Exiles 10|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=22 June 2023}}</ref> On 27 June 2013, Dobson signed a one-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] starting in 2013 to return to the [[National Rugby League]] in Australia.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Knights-sign-Michael-Dobson/78161 |title=Knights sign Michael Dobson – Newcastle Knights |access-date=2013-08-03 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130811144226/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Knights-sign-Michael-Dobson/78161 |archive-date=11 August 2013}}</ref>

===Newcastle Knights===
Dobson started playing for Newcastle in the [[2014 NRL season]]. Dobson played for the Knights in the inaugural [[Auckland Nines]] series prior to the 2014 NRL season in February 2014. With an injury to the Newcastle Knights regular [[Jarrod Mullen]], Dobson made his top-grade début for Newcastle in their 30–8 defeat by [[Penrith Panthers]]. Dobson made 25 tackles in the game, the third highest on the day for the Newcastle club. Dobson also played against his former club [[Canberra Raiders]] (in a 20–26 defeat), [[Melbourne Storm]] (in a 28–20 defeat), [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] (in a 30–0 victory), [[North Queensland Cowboys]] (in a 28–2 defeat) and [[Brisbane Broncos]] (in a 48–6 defeat).

On 21 September 2014, Dobson was named at {{rlp|hb}} in the 2014 [[NSW Cup]] Team of the Year.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2014/09/21/_2014_vb_nsw_cup_team_of_the_year.html |title=2014 VB NSW Cup Team of the Year – NSWRL |access-date=21 September 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150708221216/http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2014/09/21/_2014_vb_nsw_cup_team_of_the_year.html |archive-date=8 July 2015 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 22 May 2014, it was reported that the [[Salford Red Devils]] of the [[Super League]], had recruited Dobson for the 2015 season. In the match report following Salford's 4–25 defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]], it was reported "Salford will have re-inforcements next season with former [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] player Michael Dobson arriving from Australia after a far from happy time in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]."<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/salford-red-devils-4-wigan-7161101|title = Salford Red Devils 4 Wigan Warriors 25|date = 22 May 2014}}</ref>

On 16 June 2014, Dobson officially signed a four-year contract with the Salford club starting in 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thereddevils.net/news/rugby-news/red-devils-capture-dobson-on-long-term-deal/ |title=Red Devils Capture Dobson on Long-Term Deal | the Red Devils RLFC |access-date=2014-06-17 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140715001139/http://www.thereddevils.net/news/rugby-news/red-devils-capture-dobson-on-long-term-deal/ |archive-date=15 July 2014 }}</ref>

== Coaching career ==
After returning to Australia to play for the [[Wynnum Manly Seagulls]],<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/NDU3LTIyMjEtLTUtLS0tLTM1MDktLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - RLP|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref> Dobson spent some time coaching in the junior ranks with the club, before taking up an opportunity at the [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2022/06/21/incredibly-tough-decision-brideson-and-dobson-to-farewell-wynnum-manly/|title='Incredibly tough decision': Brideson and Dobson to farewell Wynnum Manly|date=21 June 2022|website=Queensland Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2023/11/02/griffiths-appointed-knights-nsw-cup-coach/|title=Griffiths appointed Knights NSW Cup Coach|date=2 November 2023|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>
